title: Date
description: Date is a form field for single-day selection. It uses a calendar overlay on desktop and can fall back to a native date input on mobile.
intro: Use `ui-date` when users need one exact day rather than a month, week, or date-time value. It works well for booking, deadlines, confirmations, and review workflows where the day matters but the time does not.

featureSections.title: Basic date selection
featureSections.description: The default pattern is a single field that stores an ISO date and presents it through a readable input plus calendar picker.

featureSections.title: Size and input variant
featureSections.description: Date follows the shared field system, so density and shell styling should stay aligned with the rest of the form rather than behaving like a special-case widget.

featureSections.title: Min and max constraints
featureSections.description: Use a constrained date window when users can book or submit only inside a known operating range.

featureSections.title: Readonly, disabled, and required states
featureSections.description: State handling is often more important than the picker itself, so the showcase should demonstrate realistic form states.

featureSections.title: Reactive form integration
featureSections.description: A good showcase should include validation and submit flow instead of only isolated picker chrome.

featureSections.title: Booking panel composition
featureSections.description: A practical date field often sits inside a booking or review panel with status context and follow-up actions around the chosen day.

accessibility.title: Input and popup behavior
accessibility.body:
`ui-date` behaves like a text field on desktop and can fall back to a native `type="date"` input on mobile when `useNativeOnMobile` is enabled.

| Mode | Behavior |
| --- | --- |
| desktop text input | exposes `aria-haspopup="dialog"` and `aria-expanded` |
| mobile native date input | relies on native platform date input semantics |
| disabled or readonly | prevents opening or changing the value |

accessibility.title: Keyboard
accessibility.body:
Keyboard handling follows the shared field and popup model.

| Key | Action |
| --- | --- |
| `Tab` / `Shift+Tab` | moves through the field and calendar controls |
| `Enter` / click on trigger actions | opens the date picker panel on desktop |
| button activation | selects a day or runs footer actions like clear or today |

accessibility.title: Labels and guidance
accessibility.body: Use a task-specific label such as deadline date, visit date, or confirmation date so the meaning of the chosen day is obvious. If the valid window is narrow, add helper text or nearby status text that explains the allowed range.

apiSections.title: DateComponent - ui-date
apiSections.lead: Standalone date field built on the shared field system with a calendar overlay on desktop and optional native mobile fallback.

blocks.title: Inputs
rows: Switches to a native mobile date input on small screens.

blocks.title: Inherited field inputs
rows: Shared field surface token inherited from `FieldComponent`.

blocks.title: Methods
rows: Programmatically controls the desktop picker overlay.

blocks.title: Models
rows: Works with `ngModel`, `formControl`, and `formControlName` like the other field components.
Stored ISO date string such as `2026-05-12`.

paragraphs: Use this component when one exact day matters. If users need a month, week, or date and time, use the corresponding specialized field instead.
On desktop the overlay uses the shared calendar component, while on mobile the control can defer to native date input semantics.
blocks.title: Notes
